summary: Biogen’s mid-stage trial of diranersen did not meet its main goal of showing a clear relationship between higher doses and better results.

headline: Biogen’s Alzheimer’s Drug Leaves Questions Unanswered, and That’s a Problem
summary: The presentation on  Biogen’s  experimental Alzheimer’s treatment at a medical meeting Tuesday was supposed to boost confidence in its drug’s prospects.  Instead, Biogen stock is down 6%, to $197.  Tuesday’s presentation on the drug diranersen, at London’s Alzheimer’s Association International Conference, left attendees confused about what to conclude from Biogen’s Phase 2 study known as CELIA.

headline: Biogen tumbles 8.7% as breakthrough Alzheimer’s data is clouded by dosing paradox
summary: Investing.com -- Biogen Inc. (NASDAQ:BIIB) shares fell nearly 9% following the Phase 2 CELIA readout of its highly anticipated Alzheimer’s therapy, diranersen. While the drug achieved a major scientific milestone by robustly clearing tau pathology, an unexpected inverse dose response—where the lowest dose performed the best—left Wall Street analysts confused and triggered a sell-off. Diranersen missed its primary endpoint: proving a dose-dependent response on the CDR-SB, a key measure of cogniti

headline: LEQEMBI® Real-World LEADER Study Presented at AAIC® 2026 Finds Over 75% of Early Alzheimer’s Patients Enrolled in the Study Remained Stable and Nearly 7% Improved Over an Average of 17 Months of Treatment

headline: How Investors Are Reacting To Biogen (BIIB) At-Home LEQEMBI Autoinjector Approval
summary: Eisai and Biogen recently received FDA approval for LEQEMBI IQLIK, a once-weekly subcutaneous autoinjector initiation dose that allows adults with early Alzheimer’s disease to start and continue treatment at home instead of relying solely on intravenous infusions in clinics. This at-home option, supported by data showing exposure equivalent to IV dosing and high ease-of-use ratings from patients and care partners, could meaningfully change how Alzheimer’s therapies are delivered and accessed...

headline: Biogen Presents Phase 2 CELIA Data at AAIC Demonstrating Meaningful Clinical Outcomes and Robust Tau Reduction with Diranersen in Early Alzheimer’s Disease
summary: Clinical outcomes: Diranersen demonstrated efficacy across all studied doses at 18 months, with consistent results across multiple prespecified clinical endpoints; the 60 mg dose showed the strongest response with slowing of clinical decline on the cognitive endpoints—42% on ADAS-Cog13 and 50% on MMSE—alongside a 26% slowing on CDR-SBBiomarker response: Diranersen is the first tau-directed therapy to demonstrate robust reductions in both CSF total tau, with mean reductions of 50–65%, and brain t

headline: 1 Mid-Cap Stock to Target This Week and 2 We Find Risky
summary: Mid-cap stocks have the best odds of scaling into $100 billion corporations thanks to their tested business models and large addressable markets. But the many opportunities in front of them attract significant competition, spanning from industry behemoths with seemingly infinite resources to small, nimble players with chips on their shoulders.
